title: Admin successfully creates a new student account
expected_result: HTTP 201 response; new student account record exists in the database with role 'student' and active status.

title: Admin successfully creates a new admin account
expected_result: HTTP 201 response; new account record exists in the database with role 'admin' and active status.

title: Non-admin user is rejected when attempting to create an account
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; no new account record is created in the database.

title: Admin is rejected when creating an account with a duplicate username
expected_result: HTTP 409 response; only one account record with username 'alice' exists in the database.

title: Admin is rejected when creating an account with a missing required field
expected_result: HTTP 400 response with a validation error indicating 'username' is required; no account is created.

title: Admin successfully modifies a student account email
preconditions: Student account with id 'stu-1' exists with email 'old@example.com'
expected_result: HTTP 200 response; the account record in the database has email 'new@example.com'.

title: Admin successfully modifies a user account username
preconditions: Account with id 'stu-2' exists with username 'oldname'
expected_result: HTTP 200 response; the account record in the database has username 'newname'.

title: Admin modification of non-existent account returns not-found
expected_result: HTTP 404 response; no database record is created or modified.

title: Account modification with empty body is rejected
steps: PATCH /api/admin/users/stu-3 with an empty JSON body {}
expected_result: HTTP 400 response indicating at least one modifiable field must be provided; the database record is unchanged.

title: Admin successfully deactivates an active student account
preconditions: Active student account with id 'stu-4' exists
expected_result: HTTP 200 response; the account record in the database has status 'deactivated'; all other record fields are preserved.

title: Admin deactivation of an already-deactivated account is idempotent
expected_result: HTTP 200 or 409 response; account record status remains 'deactivated'; no data is deleted.

title: Non-admin cannot deactivate an account
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; the account status in the database remains 'active'.

title: Deactivated account login attempt is rejected
expected_result: HTTP 401 or 403 response; no session token is issued; error message indicates the account is inactive.

title: Deactivated admin account login is rejected regardless of role
steps: POST /api/admin/users with body {"username":"admin-b","role":"admin","email":"admin-b@example.com"} to create admin account
POST /api/admin/users/{admin-b_id}/deactivate to deactivate the newly created admin account
expected_result: HTTP 401 response; no session token is issued; the admin role does not bypass the deactivation check.

title: Password reset token is delivered to registered contact
preconditions: Active account with username 'bob' has registered email 'bob@example.com'
steps: Inspect the outbound notification queue or test mail sink for a message addressed to 'bob@example.com'
expected_result: HTTP 202 response; exactly one password-reset credential is delivered to 'bob@example.com' via the out-of-band channel.

title: Password reset request for unknown username does not reveal existence
expected_result: HTTP 202 response with the same generic message as a successful request; no reset token is generated or delivered.

title: Reset token is delivered only to the registered contact, not returned in the API response
preconditions: Active account with username 'carol' has registered email 'carol@example.com'
expected_result: The HTTP response body does not contain the reset token value; the token is present only in the out-of-band delivery.

title: Password reset token can be used exactly once
preconditions: A valid password-reset token 'tok-abc' has been issued for 'dave'
expected_result: First confirm returns HTTP 200 and updates the password. Second confirm returns HTTP 400 or 410; token is no longer valid.

title: Expired password reset token is rejected
preconditions: A password-reset token 'tok-expired' for 'eve' has passed its expiry time
expected_result: HTTP 400 or 410 response; the account password is not changed; the token record is marked invalid.

title: Already-used reset token cannot be reused after successful reset
preconditions: Password-reset token 'tok-used' is stored in the database with status 'consumed'
steps: Call the token-validation function with token value 'tok-used'
expected_result: The function returns an invalid-token error; password update is not invoked.

title: Authorized deletion permanently removes all student personal data
preconditions: Student account with id 'stu-8' exists with associated profile, course, and submission records
steps: Query the database for any records referencing 'stu-8' across all personal data tables
expected_result: HTTP 200 response; no personal data rows referencing 'stu-8' exist in the database after deletion.

title: Deletion request for non-existent student returns not-found
expected_result: HTTP 404 response; no database state is changed.

title: Non-admin cannot delete a student account
expected_result: HTTP 403 response; the account and all associated records remain in the database.

title: Deletion of student with no associated records removes only the account row
preconditions: Student account with id 'stu-10' exists with no course, submission, or grade records
steps: Query the database for any row referencing 'stu-10'
expected_result: HTTP 200 response; no rows referencing 'stu-10' remain in any table.

title: Deleting Student A does not cascade-delete or corrupt Student B records
preconditions: Student A (id 'stu-11') has active courses, submissions, grades, and chat history
Student B (id 'stu-12') has active courses, submissions, grades, and chat history
expected_result: HTTP 200 response for Student A deletion; Student B's courses, submissions, grades, and chat history are all accessible and unaltered after Student A's deletion.
